sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 3.17 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.3)

statement.latex: a) Is a non-abelian group with a unique linear ordering necessarily simple? Is a non-commutative orderable group simple if it has no non-trivial normal relatively convex subgroups? { A.\,I.\,Kokorin} Not always, in both cases (V.\,V.\,Bludov, Algebra and Logic, { 13} (1974), 343--360).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
